steps.prompt: Which word is the finite verb?

hints.to eat: 'to eat' is 'edere' — 'est' here is from 'esse' (to be)
hints.to accept: 'to accept' is 'accipere' — 'est' is the copula verb 'to be'

hints.perfect: Perfect of 'esse' would be 'fuit' — 'est' is present tense
hints.imperfect: Imperfect of 'esse' would be 'erat' — 'est' is present
hints.future: Future of 'esse' would be 'erit' — 'est' is present

hints.sto, stare, steti, statum: These are principal parts of 'sto' (to stand)
hints.edo, edere, edi, esum: These are principal parts of 'edo' (to eat)
hints.sino, sinere, sivi, situm: These are principal parts of 'sino' (to allow)

hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'prophetam' — 'propheta' is nominative (predicate noun)
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'propheta' in form, but here it is a predicate nominative
hints.vocative: Vocative would also be 'propheta' but this is a statement, not an address

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'prophetae' ends in -ae
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'prophetae' ends in -ae
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -us — 'prophetae' ends in -ae
